html_body:
QSC Core Health Analysis Report

Core: COS-Core-24f
Analysis Period: 2026-06-07 17:51 UTC to 2026-06-08 17:51 UTC (24 hours)
Report Generated: 2026-06-08 23:21 IST

1. CORE IDENTITY

Core Device: COS-Core-24f
Monitored Processes: audio_engine, runtime_engine, video_engine, node, engine_discover
Data Sources: Prometheus (grafanacloud-prom), Loki (grafanacloud-logs)
Analysis Window: Last 24 hours (2026-06-07 17:51 UTC - 2026-06-08 17:51 UTC)

2. SYSTEM RESOURCE SUMMARY

ResourcePeak ValueAverageStatus
CPU Utilization (Peak Process)12.01%7.68%HEALTHY
Memory Utilization (Peak Process)2.48%1.47%WARNING
Memory Usage (Absolute Peak)397.36 MB235-372 MBCRITICAL
Process Restarts10 processesMultiple PIDsWARNING

Key Finding: Node process (PID 9587) shows aggressive memory growth (+107% in 6.4 hours), indicating potential memory leak. Coordinated multi-process restart event occurred at 2026-06-08 11:30 UTC.

3. CPU STATE BREAKDOWN

ProcessPIDAvg CPU %Peak CPU %DurationStatus

audio_engine11317~10%11.6%Recent spikeHEALTHY
audio_engine11338~10%10.8%Recent spikeHEALTHY
runtime_engine261653.78%4.82%15.25hHEALTHY
node89381.30%N/A16.83hHEALTHY
video_engineAll PIDs<0.1%<0.1%VariousHEALTHY

Analysis: CPU utilization is dominated by audio_engine processes, with sustained baseline around 7-8% and periodic spikes to 12%. All processes show zero wait-state CPU, indicating no I/O blocking issues. No CPU-related anomalies detected.

4. MEMORY STATE BREAKDOWN

ProcessPIDAvg Memory %Peak Memory %Absolute MemoryTrendStatus
runtime_engine261652.32%2.39%372.7 MBStableHEALTHY
node95871.84%2.48%191→397 MB+107% GrowthCRITICAL
node89381.47%1.50%235.2 MBElevated baselineWARNING
runtime_engine133681.48%1.50%236.7 MBStable (brief)HEALTHY
audio_engine255671.16%1.16%186.5 MBFlatHEALTHY
video_engine97730.364%0.468%75→42 MB-44% DeclineHEALTHY
engine_discover104990.082%0.083%13.14 MBMinimalHEALTHY

Critical Finding: node process PID 9587 demonstrates aggressive memory growth from 191.4 MB at 11:30 UTC to 397.36 MB at 17:55 UTC (+206 MB in 6.4 hours). This represents a 107% increase and is the most critical leak indicator on this core.

5. PROCESS-LEVEL ANALYSIS

  • audio_engine: Three active PIDs (25567, 11317, 11338) with consistent CPU usage (7-12%). Memory stable at 184-186 MB. Thread count constant at 12. No anomalies detected.
  • runtime_engine: Two PIDs (26165, 13368) showing highest memory consumption (372.7 MB, 236.7 MB). High thread count (191-193 threads). CPU usage moderate (3.78% avg). Stable operation.
  • video_engine: Multiple PIDs with minimal CPU (<0.1%) and declining memory footprint (75 MB → 42 MB). Thread counts elevated (55-62) but declining. Process refresh cycle detected.
  • node: CRITICAL - PID 9587 shows active memory leak (+107% growth). PID 8938 maintains elevated baseline. Thread count stable at 13.
  • engine_discover: Single-threaded process (PID 10499) with minimal resource usage (13.14 MB, <1% CPU). Operating normally.

6. MEMORY LEAK DETECTION

ProcessPIDStart MemoryEnd MemoryGrowthTime PeriodRisk Level
node9587191.4 MB397.36 MB+206 MB (+107%)6.4 hoursCRITICAL LEAK
node9602189.61 MB257.16 MB+67.5 MB (+35%)0.83 hoursHIGH RISK
runtime_engine13368223.58 MB239.83 MB+16.25 MB (+7%)0.5 hoursMONITOR
runtime_engine26165362.3 MB383.96 MBStable plateau15.25 hoursNO LEAK
audio_engineAll PIDs184 MB186.5 MBZero variance15+ hoursNO LEAK

Leak Assessment: Node process PID 9587 exhibits textbook memory leak behavior with sustained linear growth over 6.4 hours. At current rate (~32 MB/hour), this process will consume 1 GB additional memory within 20 hours if not restarted.

7. CRASH AND STACKTRACE ANALYSIS

Fatal Error Logs: No fatal stacktrace errors detected in the last 24 hours.

Loki Query Results: Query for {service_name="stacktrace", instance="COS-Core-24f"} |= "fatal" returned zero results, indicating no crash-level events were logged during the analysis period.

Interpretation: Despite the coordinated process restart event at 11:30 UTC, no fatal errors were logged. This suggests the restarts were planned maintenance or orchestrated updates rather than crash-induced failures.

8. METRICS AND LOG CORRELATION

TimestampEventAffected ProcessesMetrics ImpactLog Evidence
2026-06-08 10:40-11:00 UTCPhase 1 Restartsaudio_engine (11338), runtime_engine (13397), video_engine (10430), node (9602)Uptime reset to <200sNo fatal logs
2026-06-08 11:30 UTCPhase 2 Coordinated Restart10 processes (audio_engine, runtime_engine, engine_discover, node, video_engine)Simultaneous uptime reset across all enginesNo fatal logs
2026-06-08 11:30-17:55 UTCPost-restart Memory Growthnode (PID 9587)191 MB → 397 MB (+107%)No error logs

Correlation Analysis: The restart events show clean phased rollout without accompanying crash logs, suggesting planned maintenance. However, the aggressive memory growth in node PID 9587 post-restart indicates a potential regression or leak introduced after the restart.

9. UPTIME AND RESTART ANALYSIS

Pre-Restart Stability (2026-06-07 17:51 - 2026-06-08 09:05 UTC):

  • audio_engine PID 25567: 15.25 hours continuous operation
  • runtime_engine PID 26165: 15.25 hours continuous operation
  • node PID 8938: 16.83 hours continuous operation
  • engine_discover PID 10499: 16.83 hours continuous operation
  • video_engine PIDs 9772/9773: 16.83 hours continuous operation

Restart Event Timeline:

  1. Phase 1 (10:40-11:00 UTC): Early restart of 4 processes
  2. 50-minute gap: No new process starts
  3. Phase 2 (11:30 UTC): Coordinated restart of 10 processes across all engine types

Post-Restart Stability (2026-06-08 11:30 - 17:55 UTC):

  • All restarted processes: 6.4 hours stable operation
  • No subsequent crashes or restarts detected
  • Linear uptime growth with zero gaps

Conclusion: The restart pattern indicates planned maintenance with phased rollout. No cascading failures post-restart.

10. ANOMALIES

AnomalySeverityDetails
Memory Leak - node PID 9587CRITICAL107% memory growth in 6.4 hours (191 MB → 397 MB). Linear escalation pattern. No error logs correlate with growth.
Rapid Memory Growth - node PID 9602HIGH35% growth in 50 minutes before restart. Suggests pre-restart memory pressure contributed to maintenance event.
Coordinated Multi-Process RestartMEDIUM10 processes restarted simultaneously at 11:30 UTC. No crash logs but indicates system-wide intervention required.
Video Engine Memory DeclineINFO45% memory reduction (75 MB → 42 MB) across video_engine processes. Likely optimization or process refresh cycle.

11. SUMMARY AND RISK ASSESSMENT

Overall Status: CRITICAL

Primary Risk: Active memory leak in node process (PID 9587) with 107% growth over 6.4 hours post-restart. At current rate, this process will exhaust available memory within 20 hours.

Secondary Concerns:

  • Coordinated restart event at 11:30 UTC affecting all core processes suggests system-wide stability issue
  • Node process PID 9602 showed 35% memory growth in 50 minutes pre-restart, indicating recurring pattern
  • Runtime_engine maintains highest sustained memory baseline (372.7 MB) but stable

Positive Indicators:

  • No fatal crashes or stacktrace errors in 24-hour period
  • CPU utilization healthy across all processes (peak 12%)
  • Post-restart processes show stable operation (6.4 hours without failure)
  • Audio and video engine processes operating normally

Risk Trajectory: Without intervention, node PID 9587 will require restart within 12-20 hours. Pattern suggests this is a recurring issue that triggered the 11:30 UTC maintenance event.

12. RECOMMENDED ACTIONS

PriorityActionTimelineOwner
P0 - URGENTRestart node process PID 9587 to prevent memory exhaustion. Capture heap dump before restart for root cause analysis.Next 4-6 hoursOperations
P0 - URGENTInvestigate node process memory leak. Review recent code changes, dependencies, or configuration updates deployed around 2026-06-08 11:30 UTC.24 hoursEngineering
P1 - HIGHEnable continuous memory monitoring for node processes with alerts at 300 MB and 500 MB thresholds.48 hoursObservability
P1 - HIGHReview restart event logs from 2026-06-08 11:30 UTC to determine root cause (planned vs. forced maintenance).48 hoursOperations
P2 - MEDIUMOptimize runtime_engine memory baseline (currently 372.7 MB sustained). Evaluate memory pooling or caching strategies.1 weekEngineering
P2 - MEDIUMImplement automated restart policy for node processes when memory exceeds 400 MB to prevent service degradation.1 weekPlatform

Monitoring Recommendations:

  • Set alert threshold: node memory > 350 MB (current: 397 MB)
  • Monitor node memory growth rate: alert if >20 MB/hour sustained
  • Track process restart frequency: alert if >2 restarts per 24h window
  • Correlate Loki stacktrace logs with Prometheus uptime resets for crash detection

Report generated by QSC Sleestack Observability Platform | Grafana Assistant Health Analysis Agent
Data Sources: Prometheus (grafanacloud-prom), Loki (grafanacloud-logs) | Analysis Period: 24 hours
